outcome_text: System requirements (e.g., human-AI teaming configurations, testing, safety and performance goals) and design decisions are documented based on an AI system's intended purpose and context of use.
scoring_text: Skill in documenting AI system requirements and design decisions, including specifying human-AI teaming configurations, defining testing protocols, establishing safety and performance goals, and tracing design decisions to intended purpose and operational context.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in documenting AI system requirements and design decisions, including specifying human-AI teaming configurations, defining testing protocols, establishing safety and performance goals, and tracing design decisions to intended purpose and operational context.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in developing AI system documentation including model cards, system cards, and datasheets for datasets that meet both internal governance review and external regulatory conformity assessment requirements.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-1.6 (MAP) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.684, cosine: 0.704). Secondary: RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.672), RM-T-003 (RM, cosine: 0.665).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.684). Shared vocabulary: [ai, requirements, skill, system]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [configurations, context, decisions, defining, design, documenting] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.873 (P=0.875, R=0.871). Balanced P/R — the 32-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.704 ≈ STS 0.684 (gap 0.020). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, requirements, skill, system] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, requirements, skill, system]. NIST AI RMF-only: [configurations, context, decisions, defining, design, documenting]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing].
